Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Production Of Documents By Defendants Person(S) Most Qualified, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-07-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Larry Castruita, Esq. CASTRUITA LAW FIRM PC (949) 535-1800 lcastruita@cctrialfactory.com Attorneys for Plaintiff(s) ROZELL JONES, JR.
Kelli A. Herzog, Esq., GROTEFELD HOFFMANN LLP (415) 344-9670 kherzog@ghlaw-llp.com Attorney for Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument Court adopts the tentative ruling as modified:

PLAINTIFF ROZELL JONES Motion To Compel Deposition Attendance And Production Of Documents By Defendants Person(S) Most Qualified, And Request For Monetary Sanctions

The Court agrees with Plaintiff's assertion that "This Court has the authority and opportunity to ensure this matter can be expeditiously resolved by simply following the code and encouraging the parties to conduct discovery in a timely manner in an effort to encourage expeditious settlement. This would limit unnecessary time, Court resources and billing of unnecessary attorneys' fees and costs from unnecessarily dragging this matter longer than it needs to be."

However, the Court is troubled by the lack of civility by both parties and the fact that this simple issue requires Court intervention. Query whether Court intervention would have been sought were this not a fee shifting case.

There is nothing in the Code that mandates the time for a deposition other than a hold at the outset of litigation and the mandates of discovery cut off. Plaintiff set forth prejudice to him in delaying the deposition to 15 months before trial. Defendant has not averred that its PMK is not available until October 2024.

The Motion is denied as follows: The parties are ordered to meet and confer in good faith to arrive at a reasonable deposition date well before October 2024.

No sanctions are awarded.

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare and sumbit a proposed order tracking the court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 to Judge Pro Tem Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
